Bills passed by the assembly are given [royal assent](/wiki/Royal_assent "Royal assent") by the [lieutenant governor of Saskatchewan](/wiki/Lieutenant_governor_of_Saskatchewan "Lieutenant governor of Saskatchewan"), in the name of the [King of Canada](/wiki/King_of_Canada "King of Canada").[[1]](#cite_note-1) The assembly meets at the [Saskatchewan Legislative Building](/wiki/Saskatchewan_Legislative_Building "Saskatchewan Legislative Building") in [Regina](/wiki/Regina,_Saskatchewan "Regina, Saskatchewan").

The **Legislative Assembly of Saskatchewan** ([French](/wiki/French_language "French language"): *Assemblée législative de la Saskatchewan*) is the legislative chamber of the [Saskatchewan Legislature](/wiki/Saskatchewan_Legislature "Saskatchewan Legislature") in the province of [Saskatchewan](/wiki/Saskatchewan "Saskatchewan"), Canada. Bills passed by the assembly are given [royal assent](/wiki/Royal_assent "Royal assent") by the [lieutenant governor of Saskatchewan](/wiki/Lieutenant_governor_of_Saskatchewan "Lieutenant governor of Saskatchewan"), in the name of the [King of Canada](/wiki/King_of_Canada "King of Canada").[[1]](#cite_note-1) The assembly meets at the [Saskatchewan Legislative Building](/wiki/Saskatchewan_Legislative_Building "Saskatchewan Legislative Building") in [Regina](/wiki/Regina,_Saskatchewan "Regina, Saskatchewan").

There are 61 constituencies in the province, which elect [members of the Legislative Assembly](/wiki/Members_of_the_Legislative_Assembly "Members of the Legislative Assembly") (MLAs). All are single-member districts, though the cities of [Regina](/wiki/Regina_City_(provincial_electoral_district) "Regina City (provincial electoral district)"), [Saskatoon](/wiki/Saskatoon_City_(provincial_electoral_district) "Saskatoon City (provincial electoral district)") and [Moose Jaw](/wiki/Moose_Jaw_City "Moose Jaw City") were in the past represented through multi-member districts, with members elected through [block voting](/wiki/Multiple_non-transferable_vote "Multiple non-transferable vote").

The legislature has been unicameral since its establishment; there has never been a provincial upper house.

The [30th Saskatchewan Legislature](/wiki/30th_Saskatchewan_Legislature "30th Saskatchewan Legislature") was elected at the [2024 Saskatchewan general election](/wiki/2024_Saskatchewan_general_election "2024 Saskatchewan general election").

## Current Executive Council/Cabinet

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Legislative_Assembly_of_Saskatchewan&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Current Executive Council/Cabinet")]

For current cabinet see [Executive Council of Saskatchewan](/wiki/Executive_Council_of_Saskatchewan "Executive Council of Saskatchewan").

## Officers

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Legislative_Assembly_of_Saskatchewan&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Officers")]

In September 2013 the assembly established the position of [Usher of the Black Rod](/wiki/Usher_of_the_Black_Rod_(Canada) "Usher of the Black Rod (Canada)").[[3]](#cite_note-3) Their role is functionally similar to the [one](/wiki/Usher_of_the_Black_Rod_of_the_Senate_of_Canada "Usher of the Black Rod of the Senate of Canada") for the [Senate of Canada](/wiki/Senate_of_Canada "Senate of Canada"). Rick Mantey was the first person to hold the office. The current Usher of the Black Rod, as of 2014, is Ben Walsh.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

The Black Rod was made by Scott Olson Goldsmith of Regina.[[5]](#cite_note-5)
